data.title: IBIT ETF sees surge in inflows as bitcoin nears all-time high
data.description:
The iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (NASDAQ:IBIT), managed by BlackRock, has emerged as a powerhouse in the ETF world, drawing massive investor attention as bitcoin (BTC-USD) hovers near record highs. As of today, bitcoin (BTC-USD) is trading near $108.2K, just shy of its all-time high of $112K reached on May 22.

IBIT has seen an extraordinary surge in interest, with nearly $9B in net inflows over the past month. In fact, the fund has recorded inflows on 29 of the last 30 trading days, and has impressively avoided outflows for 32 consecutive sessions. This week alone, the ETF attracted $482M on May 28 and $411M on May 27. 

With $72B in assets under management, IBIT now ranks as the 23rd largest ETF in the U.S.—an astonishing feat for a product launched just one year ago. For comparison, the next youngest fund in the top 25 from an assets under management perspective is 12 years old. 

Year-to-date, IBIT is up 14.6%, fueled by both bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) price momentum and surging investor demand. As digital assets gain mainstream traction, IBIT’s rapid ascent underscores growing institutional confidence in cryptocurrency-based investment products. 

For reference, here are other spot bitcoin ETFs that may be worth monitoring: (BATS:ARKB), (NYSEARCA:GBTC), (NASDAQ:BRRR), (BATS:BTCO), (BATS:HODL), (BATS:BTCW), (BATS:FBTC), (NYSEARCA:BITB), and (BATS:EZBC).

data.title: First Trust Balanced Income ETF launched
data.description:
Exchange-traded funds provider and asset manager First Trust Advisors announced on Thursday the launch of the First Trust Balanced Income ETF (NYSEARCA:FTBI).

FTBI tracks the Bloomberg Moderate Allocation Income Focus Index, which is reconstituted and rebalanced semi-annually.

data.title: Performance Food Group gains again after its Investor Day presentation impresses
data.description:
Performance Food Group Company (NYSE:PFGC) traded higher for a second consecutive session after the company hosted its Investor Day event.

Highlights of the presentation from management included procurement savings, white space opportunities, e-commerce potential, and the upside in the specialty business.

In terms of guidance, Performance Food Group Company (NYSE:PFGC) said it continues to expect net sales to be in a range of $63 billion to $63.5 billion in FY25 and adjusted EBITDA to be in a range of $1.725 billion to $1.75 billion. As previously disclosed, the company's outlook for FY25 includes expected business results for Cheney Brothers as of the close of the transaction.

Looking ahead three years, the food company expects to achieve annual sales of $73 billion to $75 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $2.3 billion to $2.5 billion in FY28.

Notably, Performance Food Group's (PFGC) board authorized a new share repurchase program for up to $500 million. The authorization replaces the previously authorized $300 million share repurchase program

On Wall Street, BMO Capital Markets reiterated its Outperform rating on PFGC and $105 target price. The stock was called its top pick in the food distributor sector. UBS analyst Michael Lasser said the company laid out a compelling plan for growth. He also said Performance Food Group (PFGC) is achieving impressive results in quietly improving its technology offerings and back end systems, which the firm thinks will help with customer satisfaction over the long haul. 

Shares of Performance Food Group Company (PFGC) rose 1.0% in early trading on Thursday, after rallying more than 4% on Wednesday.

data.title: Talkspace rises on deal with Amazon Pharmacy
data.description:
• Talkspace (NASDAQ:TALK) said on Thursday that it is partnering with Amazon Pharmacy (NASDAQ:AMZN) to help its members fill their prescriptions through the provider and get faster, free home delivery.
  • In order to streamline the process, the deal lets the psychiatric provider of a Talkspace member send prescriptions directly to Amazon Pharmacy.
  • Through Amazon’s health benefits connector, shoppers can also confirm their insurance covers Talkspace therapy or psychiatry and book a virtual visit with a licensed provider, usually within 1 to 5 days, the company added.
  • TALK stock is up 14% in morning trade.

data.title: Peter Navarro on tariff court ruling: courts are ‘engaged in attacks on the American people’
data.description:
Senior White House trade adviser Peter Navarro defended the administration’s use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEPA) to impose tariffs, despite a recent court ruling against the measure.

In a Bloomberg Surveillance Podcast interview, Navarro emphasized that the tariffs were implemented to address what he described as “an economic emergency” related to fentanyl trafficking from China and “unfair trade practices.”

“There’s no question that there’s an economic emergency…and we took this step to stop that,” he said.

On Wednesday, a three-judge panel at the New York-based U.S. Court of International Trade blocked President Donald Trump from imposing most of his tariff agenda by using the IEPA law. Navarro expressed frustration with the judiciary’s intervention, suggesting that courts were undermining efforts to protect American interests.

“We’ve got courts in this country who are basically engaged in attacks on the American people,” he said. “The judiciary in this country has been weaponized in ways which are contrary to their interests.”

Despite the setback, Navarro indicated that the administration would appeal the ruling while exploring alternative legal pathways to implement the tariffs. He added that “any trade lawyer knows there are just a number of different options we can take…there are all sorts of things we can do well within the law.”

Navarro concluded with a message to international trading partners, emphasizing the administration’s determination to pursue fair trade practices regardless of legal challenges.

“I want to say this to the world. You’re cheating us. We’re coming after you. Deal. And let’s make this right,” he declared. “Because ultimately, what’s at stake here is the global international environment getting in a way where it’s fair to America.”

More on tariffs and U.S. politics:

  • Trade court strikes down Trump's April tariffs
  • Four ways Trump can re-impose tariffs struck down by trade court
  • What's next after U.S. trade court strikes down Trump tariffs?
  • Nasdaq, S&P, Dow gain as court strikes down Trump's April tariffs; Nvidia results bring cheer
  • Tariffs Are Ruled Illegal, Prepare For More Uncertainty

data.title: Webull announces redemption of incentive warrants
data.description:
Online investment platform operator Webull (NASDAQ:BULL) announced on Thursday the redemption of all of its outstanding incentive warrants (BULLZ), exercisable for class A shares at $10.00 per share.

The redemption date is set at June 30.

The incentive warrants were issued in connection with the close of the reverse merger with the blank check company SK Growth Opportunities (SKGRW) (SKGRU).

Webull said the warrants that remain unexercised at 5:00 PM New York City time on the redemption date will be void and no longer exercisable.

data.title: Pure Storage executes well but analysts await acceleration from AI hyperscaler deals
data.description:
Pure Storage's (NYSE:PSTG) first quarter results topped consensus estimates despite challenging economic conditions, and analysts eye potential acceleration ahead as deals with artificial intelligence hyperscalers materialize.

However, shares sank 8% during early market action on Thursday.

"Management said it didn’t see an out of the ordinary increase in XaaS adoption from customers in FQ1, though it noted that customers typically favor Evergreen One/opex models in times of macro uncertainty," said Evercore ISI analysts, led by Amit Daryanani, in an investor note. "In addition, Pure's Meta (META) hyperscale program appears to be progressing along as expected and Pure reiterated its expectation of shipping double-digit exabytes in FY27, though it didn't want to narrow in the range yet. Conversations with additional hyperscalers around proof of concepts continue to take place but no explicit win to flag (though we think this could be an upside lever in H2)."

Evercore maintained its Outperform rating and $70 price target.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley retained its Equal-weight rating and $62 price target.

"META deal is progressing as expected and remains on track for 1-2 exabytes in 2H of the year, but no major developments this quarter, as we previewed. Management noted that Pure is in POCs with other hyperscalers, which while encouraging, we believe any revenue related to another deal is further out," said Morgan Stanley analysts Meta Marshall and Mary Lenox, in an investor note. "While our checks had been cautious on the Enterprise spending environment, the Company saw minimal change in customer sentiment or pull forwards in FQ1 given the uncertain macro environment, which is more similar to what we have heard from other Enterprise exposed names."

NetApp (NTAP), which also competes in the flash storage space, was up marginally during early Thursday trading. Dell Technologies (DELL) inched down 1.2% and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) crept up 0.7%.

data.title: Mullen Automotive to enact 1-for-100 reverse stock split to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Mullen Automotive (NASDAQ:MULN) will enact 1-for-100 reverse stock split of its common stock effective June 2, 2025.
  • The stock will continue trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker “MULN” on a split-adjusted basis starting June 2, 2025.
  • The reverse split aims to help the company regain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1.00 minimum bid price requirement.
  • MULN shares fall nearly 22% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Tenable Holdings to acquire Apex Security
data.description:
• Tenable Holdings (NASDAQ:TENB) announced plans to acquire Apex Security, a startup specializing in securing the AI attack surface.
  • Apex’s tools will extend Tenable AI Aware by adding governance, policy enforcement, and exposure control for both user-adopted and custom-built AI.
  • Apex, founded in 2023, has backing from top investors including Sam Altman, Clem Delangue, Sequoia Capital, and Index Ventures.
  • Integration of Apex’s capabilities into Tenable One is expected in the second half of 2025.
  • Financial terms of the acquisition were not disclosed; the deal is expected to close later this quarter.
  • Source: Press release
